ULCC.title: Spirit Collapse Sparks Scramble As Rivals Rush In With Rescue Fares

macro_events.event_summary: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee — historically a dove — called recent inflation data "bad news," signaling the Fed is unlikely to cut rates soon and may stay restrictive longer.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer policy rates raise discount rates on long-duration assets
Sticky inflation pressures real consumer spending and rate-sensitive sectors
Hawkish shift from a known dove disproportionately moves rate expectations

beneficiaries.rationale: Money market and short-duration fixed income flows benefit asset gatherers; less direct but Moody's/rating agencies see sustained issuance activity in higher-rate regimes. Weak link — listing as low confidence.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder with entry-level buyer concentration most sensitive to mortgage rates; consensus estimates assume rate relief in 2H.
Zillow revenue tied to housing transaction volumes which freeze further when mortgage rates remain elevated; Premier Agent and mortgage segments both impacted.
Opendoor carries large inventory financed at floating rates; higher-for-longer compresses unit economics and prolongs the housing transaction freeze.

headlines: Recent inflation data was 'bad news,' Fed's Goolsbee says
